---
title: "Materials"
---
{{< include _in-progress.qmd >}}

## Slides

- [Introduction to the workshop](slides/1-intro.html){target="_blank"}
- [The `samplr` package](slides/2-samplr.html){target="_blank"}
- [The Autocorrelated Bayesian Sampler](slides/3-abs.html){target="_blank"}
- [Approximate Bayesian Computation (+ Discussion)](slides/4-abc.html){target="_blank"}

## Files
Practice during the workshop will make use of [these files](slides/files_for_attendees.zip).
Part 2 data [here](slides/files_for_attendees/data.zip)

## Documentation to the `samplr` package
The `samplr` package is an R package available on [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/samplr/index.html).
With it, you can: 

- use MCMC algorithms
- use the Autocorrelated Bayesian Sampler model [@zhu2024AutocorrelatedBayesianSampler]
- calculate some useful statistics that help with model inference.


The documentation to the `samplr` package lives [here](https://lucas-castillo.github.io/samplr/) (with some very useful [vignettes](https://lucas-castillo.github.io/samplr/articles/)!). We have also written a manuscript on how to use it and how to fit MCMC models [@castillo2025SamplrPackageTool]. 

It is an open source package, meaning that you can look at the source code [here](https://github.com/lucas-castillo/samplr). 

If you have any feature suggestions or if you find any bugs, please report them [here](https://github.com/lucas-castillo/samplr/issues) (or send Lucas Castillo an email).
